claim: Per-gene essentiality classification for 455 Syn3A genes from Breuer 2019 (as redistributed in the Luthey-Schulten Minimal_Cell_ComplexFormation Comparative Proteomics sheet). Three classes: Essential (270), Quasiessential (113), Nonessential (72).
source_detail: Labels were extracted from the 'Comparative Proteomics' sheet of initial_concentrations.xlsx (SHA-256 cbddd55af04f4ba7ac1309851aa0f4225f1ff74f3e78333992ec721aa3530dea) in the Luthey-Schulten Minimal_Cell_ComplexFormation repo; that file is Luthey-Schulten's distillation of Breuer 2019 Fig. 4 / Supp. Table. Both sources are cited because the file is staged locally but the underlying science is Breuer 2019.
caveats: Only 455 of the 458 CDS are labeled (3 CDS from CP016816.2 have no label in this sheet); Layer 6 must decide how to handle unlabeled genes (drop vs. treat as unknown).

Original Breuer 2019 paper reports 452 genes in their metabolic FBA model - that is a subset of this table; the broader 455 includes non-metabolic essentials (e.g. ribosomal proteins) whose essentiality comes from Hutchison 2016 transposon data rather than the FBA model.
For binary MCC, a conventional mapping is {Essential, Quasiessential} -> positive, {Nonessential} -> negative. This yields 383 positives / 72 negatives.
Breuer 2019's reported FBA MCC of 0.59 is against this three-class mapping; match the mapping carefully when benchmarking.
